text.title: Agentic ideation pipeline: generation, dedup, cluster, score
text.summary: Three-pass pipeline: diverge (generation only, no evaluation), semantic dedup, cluster-and-score. Each pass is a separate prompt or function call — never combined in one.

rule.statement: Send the generation prompt with only the problem statement and constraints. Do NOT include scoring criteria, evaluation language, or ranking instructions. The output must be a numbered list with no justification text.
rule.rationale: Including evaluation language in the generation prompt causes premature convergence — the model self-censors and produces fewer, safer ideas than an unconstrained generation pass.

rule.statement: Seed the generation pass with persona injection to force diverse viewpoints: "Generate from the perspective of a skeptic, an optimist, a first-time user, and an engineer." This produces more diverse output than an anonymous generation pass.
rule.rationale: Agent-generated ideas plateau in novelty around 30-40 without persona switching. Training data bias causes clustering around the most common solutions in the domain.

rule.statement: Remove near-duplicate ideas before clustering using semantic similarity (cosine similarity threshold 0.82 with sentence-transformers). Do not rely on exact string matching — paraphrased duplicates survive it.
rule.rationale: Duplicate ideas inflate cluster size and give false confidence in idea frequency. A cluster with 8 ideas that are all paraphrases of one idea is not 8 independent signals.

rule.statement: Cluster deduplicated ideas into 5-7 semantic themes in a separate pass. Score each cluster on Impact (3x weight) and Feasibility (2x weight). Return a scored shortlist of top 5-10 clusters for human review.
rule.rationale: Scoring before clustering produces per-idea scores that ignore the compound value of a theme. Scoring after clustering evaluates the best implementation of each direction.

rule.statement: Require human review of cluster boundaries before finalizing scores. The clustering pass can silently merge two distinct viable approaches that share domain vocabulary.
rule.rationale: Semantic clustering by cosine similarity groups by surface word overlap, not conceptual distinctiveness. Two approaches can share words while being strategically opposed.

rule.statement: For risk surfacing: invert the problem ("How would we make X fail?"), generate negative ideas, then reverse each into a positive mitigation. Run as two sequential passes — inversion then reversal — not combined.
rule.rationale: Groups generate more honest and specific negative ideas than positive ones, especially when they are politically constrained from criticizing directly.
